hints: The planned trip for the near future is now expressed in the present continuous.

a1.are taking: There is a specific schedule for the near future, so we use present continuous: "are taking".

hints: It refers to a completed success in the past, so Past Simple is needed.

a1.did: We describe the result in the past using Past Simple: "did really well".

hints: The action will be ongoing at a specific moment in the future — we use Future Continuous.

a1.will be working: By the time of the next meeting, the action will be in progress, so Future Continuous: "will be working".

hints: For duration until a certain moment in the future, we use Future Perfect Continuous.

a1.will have been studying: By the time of completing the master's degree, the process will have lasted six years, so Future Perfect Continuous.

hints: The result that is important now is expressed in Present Perfect.

a1.has found: She already has a result that is important now — we use Present Perfect: "has found".
